insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change provides more flexibility in querying data and can help improve the performance of storefronts. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The Storefront API documentation should be consulted for more information on using the @inContext directive with the channelId argument.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants may see improved performance and more accurate data in their storefronts as a result of this update. This change can also enable more advanced sales channel-specific features and functionality.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and control over data queries, enabling developers to build more advanced and sales channel-specific features. It also improves the performance and accuracy of storefronts.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports an optional channelId argument, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and stops those commands with an actionable error, and preserves actionable build errors during h2 deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: Create Hydrogen version 5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Shopify updates: new inventory APIs, mTLS certificate requirements, and Hydrogen improvements

themes.title: New Features and APIs
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the Storefront API now supports the `channelId` argument in the `@inContext` directive. Additionally, new fields have been added to the `ExchangeLineItem` type, and POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations for bundle components.

themes.title: Security and Authentication
themes.detail: The card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps that store cardholder data must be updated to use this certificate. This change is mandatory for all apps that use the card-deposit endpoint.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI Updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released, with improvements to non-interactive shop and storefront creation, and better error handling during deployment. The Shopify CLI has also been updated with new store commands and bug fixes.

actions.text: Enable the physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use the Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate for the card-deposit endpoint
Review and update app names to comply with the new App Store requirement 4.1.2
